name: Milk substitute (food medium)

components.name: water
components.evidence: Water 215.2 g per 244 g cup = 88.2 g/100g

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: Protein 4.3 g/244g cup; amino-acid profile below is Glu/Pro-rich = sodium caseinate

components.name: total carbohydrate
components.evidence: Carbohydrate by difference 15 g/244g cup; principal fermentable carbon pool. USDA record does NOT resolve individual sugars (all listed --)

components.name: sucrose
components.source: FooDB FOOD00848 component / USDA FDC 171264 (sugars not quantified)
components.evidence: Sucrose (or corn-syrup solids) is the typical carbohydrate carrier of lauric-oil milk substitutes, but this USDA record gives no specific-sugar breakdown; amount unquantified

components.name: total fat
components.evidence: Total lipid 8.3 g/244g; saturated 3.03 g/100g, dominated by lauric-acid (coconut/lauric) oil

components.name: iron
components.evidence: 0.95 mg/244g (single Fe pool; FooDB Fe compartment duplicates removed)

components.name: chloride
components.source: FooDB FOOD00848 component; USDA FDC 171264 (Cl not quantified)
components.evidence: Present (counter-ion, salt-fortified) but not quantified in this USDA record

components.name: sulfate
components.source: FooDB FOOD00848 component; derived from Met/Cys + ash
components.evidence: Present as sulfur pool; not separately quantified

components.name: molybdate
components.source: FooDB FOOD00848 component; USDA FDC 171264 (Mo listed --)
components.evidence: Trace micronutrient, not quantified in this record; retained at null

components.name: niacin (B3, nicotinate)
components.evidence: Reported 0 in this record; retained (FooDB component) but not a meaningful source here

components.name: vitamin A (retinol)
components.evidence: Retinol / Vitamin A RAE reported 0 in this record

components.name: vitamin D
components.source: FooDB FOOD00848 component; USDA FDC 171264 (D listed --, not measured)
components.evidence: Milk substitutes are commonly D-fortified but this USDA record does not quantify it; amount null

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace/contaminant element, not a growth nutrient; not quantified in USDA record

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: cobalt trace ion; only nutritionally relevant via B12, which is 0 in this record; unquantified trace

remove.name: Fe2+ mitochondria
remove.reason: model intracellular-compartment duplicate of iron; consolidated into single 'iron' entry

remove.name: Iron (Fe3+)
remove.reason: redox/compartment duplicate of iron; consolidated into single 'iron' entry

remove.name: O2 O2
remove.reason: environmental/model boundary metabolite (electron acceptor), not a food-derived nutrient

remove.name: CO2 CO2
remove.reason: model boundary/exchange metabolite, not a food-derived nutrient

remove.name: H+
remove.reason: model proton boundary metabolite, not a food composition nutrient

remove.name: Ammonium
remove.reason: model nitrogen boundary metabolite; not a food-intrinsic nutrient of this product

missing_added: L-phenylalanine, L-valine, L-serine (amino acids absent from FooDB list)
myristic, palmitic, stearic, oleic, linoleic acids (only C8/C10/C12 were present)
full quantitative mineral panel (K, Na, P, Ca, Mg, Zn, Mn, Cu, Se) and B1/B2 amounts
flag: individual fermentable sugars are NOT resolved by USDA for this item (total carbohydrate 6.16 g/100g only)
keep_uncovered: Ergocalciferol (vitamin D2) — fortificant, unquantified in this USDA record, no growth-relevant amount
notes: Composition maps to USDA 'Milk substitutes, fluid, with lauric acid oil' (FDC 171264 / SR Legacy NDB 01050); the lauric-dominant fatty-acid profile (coconut/lauric oil) and Glu/Pro-rich amino-acid profile (sodium caseinate) confirm the identity. Dominant carbon/energy sources are total carbohydrate (6.16 g/100g, likely sucrose/corn-syrup solids but not sugar-resolved by USDA) plus the lauric-oil fat fraction; this is an unfermented processed product so no organic acids are characteristic or reported. This specific USDA record does not quantify individual sugars, vitamin D/E/K, or several B vitamins (reported 0 or --), so those are given null/0 with notes rather than fabricated. concentration_mM left null throughout to avoid a density assumption.
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 171264 (SR Legacy, Milk substitutes, fluid, with lauric acid oil; NDB 01050)
